page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU configuration consists of 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. This server does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for its disk operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the c3-standard-8 demonstrates consistent mid-tier performance. The system achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1621.89 and a multi-core score of 6644.93, both falling within the average range. Memory performance is characterized by a high cached read bandwidth of 544.66 GB/sec for small blocks, which drops to 52.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in cryptography and vector calculations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks, scoring in the lower 25% tier.

With a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for mainstream enterprise applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without the overhead of specialized GPU or local NVMe storage hardware. The c3-standard-8 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the server consistently in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1689.0 and a multi-core score of 6526.0. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, facilitating efficient cryptography and vector operations. Memory performance is robust, delivering a cached read bandwidth of 544.66 GB/sec. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c3-standard-8 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced instruction sets, and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
